title: anthyllis dillenii subsp. praepropera
intro: Anthyllis dillenii subsp. praepropera, also known as kidney vetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving urinary tract infections, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of kidney stones. Anthyllis dillenii subsp. praepropera can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support kidney health and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary issues.
